PROJECT_CONTEXT.md.purpose: High-level repo map and durable project context.
read_when: Starting broad repo work
Explaining app architecture
Verifying setup or product context
skip_when: A narrow file-level pass already has scope and registry context

README.md.purpose: Human setup and runbook entry point.
read_when: Running the app
Checking environment commands
Helping the user self-serve
skip_when: Only source refactoring is needed

lib/README.md.purpose: Feature code map, feature-level README index, and cross-cutting code-doc routing.
read_when: Starting broad lib/ feature work
Choosing which feature-level README to open
Checking feature folder structure or cross-cutting architecture doc owners
skip_when: A narrow file-level task already has a specific owner doc

firebase/README.md.purpose: Firebase environment config, App Check, aliases, native/web config files, deploy prerequisites, and current environment state.
read_when: Switching Firebase environments
Changing native or web Firebase config
Diagnosing App Check, project registration, or deploy prerequisite issues
Checking current dev/staging/prod Firebase state
skip_when: No Firebase environment behavior is touched

docs/widget_catalog.md.purpose: Widget inventory, feature ownership map, and component-contract registry changelog.
read_when: Auditing a specific feature's widget surface
Checking whether a primitive already exists
Updating widget APIs, screen ownership models, sliver/tab structures, reusable design-system roles, or component contract coverage
skip_when: Only process rules are needed; use rules.json instead

docs/host_tooling_consolidation_tracker..purpose: Temporary active tracker for remaining host-tooling product decisions after shared host surfaces were consolidated.
read_when: Planning Edit event UX
Planning host-visible club archive or delete UX
Auditing host manage, attendance, hosted schedule, or host palette behavior
skip_when: Only checking implemented host widget ownership; use widget_catalog.md
Only checking callable ownership; use backend_operation_catalog.md

docs/public_profile_overhaul_tracker.md.purpose: Temporary active tracker for remaining cardless public-profile UX work after ProfileSurface replaced the old deck/card path.
read_when: Planning profile prompt picker UX
Expanding compatibility reasons
Adding profile quality coaching
Adding profile surface visual regression coverage
Doing device QA for Catches/ProfileSurface
Cleaning visible swipe-language copy
skip_when: Only checking schema contracts; use data_contracts.md
Only checking widget inventory; use widget_catalog.md

docs/config_cicd_platform_audit_2026-05-.purpose: Temporary active tracker for remaining config, CI/CD, platform, observability, contract-source, and environment-codegen follow-ups after the 2026-05-21 hardening pass.
read_when: Checking unfinished config or CI/CD hardening items
Verifying GA4 BigQuery, Crashlytics symbol-upload noise, analytics plist state, Razorpay env codegen, or contract-source migration decisions
Reviewing why firebase-tools, Flutter versions, contracts CI, app-build matrix, App Check activation, and Android google-services guards changed
skip_when: Only executing normal release operations; use release_operations.md
Only changing schema contracts; use data_contracts.md

docs/ui_architecture.md.purpose: Unified layout architecture guide for spacing, scroll ownership, slivers, NestedScrollView tab bodies, sticky headers, semantic UI lint policy, token tiers, design-tool workflow, component contracts, and scroll-heavy widget tests.
read_when: Changing shared screen padding, tab body insets, or sliver body gaps
Adding layout constants intended to be reused across sibling widgets
Adding or escalating Catch UI analyzer lints or design-token categories
Changing Style Dictionary adoption policy or component contract registry workflow
Changing CustomScrollView, SliverList, SliverPersistentHeader, or NestedScrollView surfaces
Debugging inconsistent spacing, blank scroll states, or overflow scroll bugs
skip_when: The task is pure business logic or repository/controller work
A local component uses a one-off spacing value that is not becoming a reusable contract
No scroll/sliver behavior is involved; use SLIVER-001 summary instead

docs/audit_registry/rules.json.purpose: Active/watch/archived recursive audit rules, including provider stream lifecycle constraints and branded error UI requirements.
read_when: Starting an audit or cleanup pass
Adding or updating recurring process rules
Checking whether widget catalog updates are required
Checking whether in-development event policy code is intentionally unused
Checking whether in-development event success code is intentionally unused
Changing realtime Firestore stream providers or listener lifecycles
Changing app-facing error presentation
skip_when: A task is a trivial one-off command

lib/event_policies/README.md.purpose: Feature-local preservation marker and migration guide for event policy bundles, preview fixtures, admission, waitlists, pricing, cancellation, settlement, and the dev-only lab.
read_when: Auditing lib/event_policies
Considering dead-code cleanup of event policy files
Reviewing deterministic host-configuration preview fixtures
Changing the dev-only event policy lab route, Settings entry, or UI
Planning the event-platform migration from runs to richer event admission, pricing, cancellation, or settlement policies
skip_when: The task does not touch event policies, run admission, waitlists, pricing, cancellation, settlement, or booking migration

lib/safety/README.md.purpose: Feature-local source of truth for blocking, reporting, account deletion, safety retention, and deletion/anonymization behavior.
read_when: Changing block or unblock flows
Changing report-user behavior
Changing account deletion or anonymization behavior
Changing safety-related Firestore rules or callable ownership
skip_when: No safety, reporting, block, or deletion behavior is touched

lib/user_profile/README.md.purpose: Feature-local source of truth for private profile identity fields, profile edit policy, public projection inputs, and remaining verified profile issues.
read_when: Changing profile edit UI
Changing phone, date of birth, gender, or display-name behavior
Changing public profile projection inputs
Planning profile schema migration
skip_when: No user-profile contract or profile edit behavior is touched

docs/event_success.md.purpose: Live event-success architecture, format mapping, product guardrails, Firestore contracts, manual QA, participant metrics, and open product decisions.
read_when: Auditing lib/event_success
Considering dead-code cleanup of event success files
Changing event-success host setup, attendee companion, or feedback/report metrics
Changing event-success Firestore collections, Functions, scorecards, or participant metrics
Running manual QA for event-success host or attendee surfaces
Planning live event facilitation, host coaching, check-in, host help, reveal, compatibility, or activity-agnostic event platform work
skip_when: The task does not touch live event success, event quality, attendee mixing, participant metrics, or post-event coaching strategy

docs/event_success_theatrical_experience.purpose: Temporary active tracker for Event Success live ceremony polish, invite-loop follow-up, private afterglow recap planning, and branded audio evaluation.
read_when: Planning or implementing Event Success live ceremony polish
Changing attendee companion moment theatre, host live showtime console, native cues, or reveal ceremony behavior
Planning Partiful-like invite sharing or private post-event recap artifacts
Evaluating whether branded audio assets should replace native cues
skip_when: Only checking durable event-success contracts; use docs/event_success.md
No event-success experience, invite, recap, or sensory behavior is touched

docs/controller_patterns.md.purpose: Current controller, view-model, and realtime stream lifecycle boundary rules.
read_when: Controller/state-management/refactor work
Moving business logic out of widgets
Changing keepAlive, autoDispose, or retained-tab stream ownership
skip_when: Pure visual primitive work

docs/action_cardinality_policy.md.purpose: Product and engineering policy for not-allowed, singleton, unbounded, and domain-bounded user actions.
read_when: Adding or changing a user-visible action
Changing a callable or repository mutation
Auditing whether a UI supports zero, one, or many records
Deciding whether a product limit is real or just a UI shortcut
skip_when: The task is purely visual and does not add, remove, enable, disable, or change an action

docs/error-handling-audit.md.purpose: Active app-wide error-management source of truth, backend hard-migration checklist, frontend/local error-management playbook, presentation descriptor contract, Error 101 guide, app error catalogue, remediation map, and branded error surface guidance.
read_when: Changing exceptions, stable error codes, backend or frontend/local error mapping, presentation descriptors, logging, analytics, or user-facing error rendering
Adding Firebase/Auth/Storage/Functions/Messaging/Remote Config/App Check operations
Adding validation, local persistence, route/deep-link, plugin/platform, provider, mutation, or global-framework error handling
Deciding whether a repeated failure deserves a new AppException subclass
Replacing raw AsyncValue error branches
skip_when: No error behavior is touched

docs/data_contracts.md.purpose: Firestore document shape, Cloud Functions write ownership, schema tooling, relationship documents, migration policy, and rules-test workflow.
read_when: Changing Firestore rules
Changing Functions mutation boundaries
Changing shared data contracts or schema-source tooling
Changing schema-generated Firestore TypeScript type generation
Changing callable validation schemas
Changing prompt catalogs or profile prompts
Changing seed validation or demo seed document builders
Changing relationship/action documents, match-scoped messages, beta reset strategy, or deletion/anonymization cleanup
Planning storage-path migrations such as swipes to profile decisions
Running or diagnosing Firestore rules tests
skip_when: Pure Flutter presentation work
Running day-to-day demo ops without changing seeded document shape

docs/backend_operation_catalog.md.purpose: Human-readable backend write ownership catalog covering direct client writes, callable-owned mutations, trigger-owned projections, server-only collections, and notification starting points.
read_when: Changing a repository write or callable interface
Changing Firestore rules for a write path
Adding Cloud Functions or trigger-owned projections
Planning notifications or activity timeline fan-out
skip_when: No backend write ownership or notification behavior is touched

docs/audit_registry/README.md.purpose: Recursive audit workflow, registry files, pruning policy, and completion criteria.
read_when: Starting any repeated cleanup/audit pass
skip_when: Task is a trivial one-off command

docs/audit_registry/backlog.json.purpose: Active backlog, next-up queue, stable debt ids, and scanner counts.
read_when: Choosing the next audit task
Updating debt status or scanner snapshots
Answering what remains pending
skip_when: A user only needs high-level prose from docs/README.md
A closed historical detail is only needed for proof; use passes.jsonl/files.jsonl instead

docs/audit_registry/doc_versions.json.purpose: Version metadata and read policies for durable docs.
read_when: Adding, deleting, renaming, archiving, or materially changing durable docs
Checking whether markdown front matter and registry metadata agree
skip_when: No durable docs or read policies changed

docs/audit_registry/doc_summaries.json.purpose: Compact read/skip policies for durable docs.
read_when: Deciding which docs to open for a task
Changing doc read policies or durable doc ownership
skip_when: The relevant doc was already identified by a stable debt id

docs/release_operations.md.purpose: Consolidated CI, release, deploy, environment, smoke-test, store-readiness, and release-evidence runbook.
read_when: Changing GitHub Actions release gates
Deploying Firebase/Functions/rules/storage changes
Preparing TestFlight or production release evidence
Checking environment setup or deploy order
Checking remaining store/product release tasks after production checklist deletion
skip_when: No CI, deploy, release, or environment behavior is touched

docs/web_surface_architecture.md.purpose: Domain/subdomain ownership, Firebase Hosting targets, stack boundaries for marketing/app/admin, marketing CI/CD, and future host-portal placement.
read_when: Changing website stacks
Changing Firebase Hosting targets or custom domains
Changing marketing website CI/CD
Planning app, marketing, admin, or host dashboard subdomains
Deciding whether host tools belong under admin, app, or a host portal
skip_when: Only changing Flutter mobile UI behavior
Only changing backend data contracts with no web routing impact

docs/plans/host_listing_discovery_archit.purpose: Organizer and event discovery architecture for source mentions, deterministic dedupe, bounded LLM extraction/adjudication, admin review, claimable listings, and Firestore projection planning.
read_when: Changing organizer or event intake workflows
Adding crawler, search, editorial, or platform-source ingestion
Designing source-mention, cluster, dedupe, LLM, or admin-review policy
Planning claimable organizer pages or external event projection
skip_when: Only changing marketing website layout with no organizer data-flow impact
Only changing Flutter mobile UI behavior

docs/location_stack_plan.md.purpose: Google Maps/Places, location permissions, run coordinates, check-in geofencing, map navigation, and map/demo readiness source of truth.
read_when: Changing location permissions
Changing Google Maps or Places usage
Changing run coordinates, check-in fences, or directions
Verifying map readiness for demo data
skip_when: No location, map, or coordinate behavior is touched

docs/marketing_landing_page_research.md.purpose: Reference-site research, corrected product-positioning brief, rejected run-only preview note, content inventory, visual direction, asset requirements, and app-theme implications for the Catch marketing website.
read_when: Redesigning the Catch marketing site
Changing launch landing-page copy or imagery
Choosing brand palette, typography, or visual direction that should inform the app
Reviewing competitor or alternative landing-page patterns for hosts, dating, or event software
Checking why the first isolated preview was rejected
skip_when: Only syncing existing app screenshots through the media manifest
Only changing backend waitlist storage or form submission behavior

docs/marketing_landing_page_tracker.md.purpose: Temporary active tracker for the marketing landing-page research, rejected isolated preview, corrected preview, production rewrite, and app visual-direction follow-up.
read_when: Checking what remains before the website rewrite
Finding reference screenshot status
Confirming whether production website files should be edited yet
Checking whether a preview is approved, rejected, or pending
skip_when: The marketing rewrite has shipped and this tracker has been folded into durable docs

docs/demo_data_seeding.md.purpose: Demo data seeding, warm accounts, manual matching, reset/cleanup, demo ops, and validation workflow.
read_when: Changing seed data generation
Warming a beta/demo account
Resetting synthetic demo state
Validating seeded run/match/check-in data
skip_when: No demo data, demo ops, or synthetic state is touched

docs/sales_demo_seed_tracker.md.purpose: Temporary active tracker for sales-grade synthetic supply, host-demo readiness, canonical persona assets, market packs, event policy coverage, and demo migration.
read_when: Changing high-fidelity demo personas or host demo seed quality
Checking sales-demo acceptance criteria
Planning image generation, persona catalog migration, or demo-surface reuse
Reviewing remaining work before a host sales demo
skip_when: No sales-demo synthetic data, persona assets, or host-demo flow is touched

docs/sales_demo_persona_cohorts.md.purpose: Cohort scope, New York and India persona-library decisions, roster reuse policy, and city overlay rules for sales-grade synthetic users.
read_when: Changing synthetic persona cohort size or market coverage
Planning New York or India demo audiences
Choosing roster overlap and reuse rules
Adding city overlays for synthetic demo events
skip_when: Only command syntax or non-persona demo seeding behavior is touched

docs/sales_demo_image_generation_runbook.purpose: Mechanical ChatGPT-web image generation, review, local organization, UID-owned Storage upload, and post-upload validation workflow.
read_when: Generating or reviewing synthetic persona images
Downloading, renaming, or normalizing ChatGPT web outputs
Uploading persona images to Firebase Storage
Validating profile photo URLs and thumbnails before live seed writes
skip_when: No synthetic persona image assets are being generated, uploaded, or published
